% ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
% This file was generated with the assistance of AI (Claude, Anthropic).
% build/title.tex
% ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
% Quarto template partial for the PDF title block.
% Copied from Quarto's default title.tex and extended with two overrides:
%   1. Callout box background — Quarto hardcodes colback=white and
%      opacityback=0 inline on every tcolorbox callout. Since inline options
%      override any global \tcbset, we wrap the tcolorbox environment itself
%      so our overrides always fire last and win.
%   2. Code block line number spacing — \fvset adjusts fancyvrb globally
%      after all environments are defined.
% ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

$if(title)$
\title{$title$$if(thanks)$\thanks{$thanks$}$endif$}
$endif$
$if(subtitle)$
\usepackage{etoolbox}
\makeatletter
\providecommand{\subtitle}[1]{% add subtitle to \maketitle
  \apptocmd{\@title}{\par {\large #1 \par}}{}{}
}
\makeatother
\subtitle{$subtitle$}
$endif$
$-- This supports Quarto's authors normalization
\author{$for(authors)$$it.name.literal$$sep$ \and $endfor$}

% ── Callout box background override ──────────────────────────────────────────
\makeatletter
\let\orig@tcolorbox\tcolorbox
\let\endorig@tcolorbox\endtcolorbox
\renewenvironment{tcolorbox}[1][]{%
  \orig@tcolorbox[#1, colback=bookcream, opacityback=1]%
}{%
  \endorig@tcolorbox%
}
\makeatother
